Open in a new tab

achallenge conducted by needle and syringe

bchallenge via mosquito bite
P. vivax CHMI studies have been conducted in Cali, Columbia; WRAIR, Bethesda, Maryland and QIMR, Brisbane

This review will focus on the establishment of P. falciparum sporozoite CHMI models in Africa, as well as the progress, current, and recent advances, and challenges of using this model in malaria exposed individuals.2. Conduct of CHMI
The Plasmodium life cycle involves two hosts: mosquitoes and humans. Within the human host, the cycle is broadly divided into three stages: (i) pre-erythrocytic stage (liver stage) (ii) erythrocytic stage (blood-stage), and (iii) sexual stage; all represent potential vaccine targets (Duffy and Patrick Gorres 2000). There are two main ways that CHMI can be initiated (Fig. 1): the sporozoite challenge; this can be carried out by the traditional inoculation of sporozoites by lab-reared mosquito bites (Epstein et al. 2007; Verhage et al. 2005) or by the direct inoculation via needle and syringe of a known amount of sporozoites (Sheehy et al. 2013; Billingsley 2014; Mordmüller 2015) and Plasmodium-infected red blood cells, so-called induced blood-stage malaria (IBSM) (Duncan and Draper 2012).
Fig. 1.

Open in a new tab
Controlled human malaria infection stages. P. falciparum life cycle begins when infected sporozoites are injected into the skin. The sporozoites are rapidly transported via the bloodstream to the hepatocytes in the liver. Within the hepatocytes, the parasite matures, differentiates, and undergoes several cycles of asexual multiplication releasing merozoites into the bloodstream. Once released the merozoites quickly infect erythrocytes and undergo synchronous cycles of asexual replication, progressing through the ring stage, trophozoite stage before finally into the schizont stage. Administration of challenge can be either through sporozoites (i) mosquito bite through the skin or (ii) needle and syringe via intradermal (ID), direct venous injection (DVI), or intramuscular (IM). Sporozoites travel to the liver where they replicate and are released into the blood. Alternatively (iii), infected red blood cells can be administered directly into the blood, bypassing the liver beginning blood stage replication2.1. Sporozoite Challenge (Mosquito-Bite and Injection Challenge Studies)
Direct inoculation with sporozoites allows for the development of both liver stage and blood-stage infection and is particularly useful for studying vaccine candidates that target the sporozoite/liver stages. Although mosquito bite challenge would be the most preferred method as it mimics natural infection it does have its limitations, the challenge dose is variable as it is not possible to control the number of sporozoites being injected by the mosquito plus the method was logistically difficult to reproduce and is restricted to only a few research centers with insectary facilities and trained staff (Epstein 2013).
Sanaria Inc., a biotechnology company in Rockville, Maryland, spent the last 10 years addressing the challenges of producing and purifying sporozoites and have developed a method that produces aseptic, purified, cryopreserved, infectious NF54 P. falciparum sporozoites (PfSPZ) for injection that complies with regulatory standards. This offers a standardized and reproducible method of conducting CHMI studies (Billingsley 2014; Roestenberg et al. 2009a, 2013; Vanderberg 2007) because of this CHMI by injectable PfSPZ is replacing the traditional method of mosquito bite challenge.
As of August 2021, more than 30 clinical trials of PfSPZ based products have been conducted in 6 sites in the U.S., in 4 European and 7 African countries with over 1200 volunteers receiving PfSPZ (NF54) challenge (Sanaria. 2012). These studies are safe, reproducible, and effective in causing infections in all volunteers (Spring et al. 2014; Epstein 2013; Jongo et al. 2019a, 2018; Draper et al. 2018; Goh et al. 2019; Steinhardt 2019; Stanisic et al. 2018). PfSPZ challenges have been administered by intradermal (ID), direct venous injection (DVI), or intramuscular (IM) all routes have shown to be safe, well-tolerated, and infectious, with DVI being the most efficient route (Roestenberg et al. 2013; Steinhardt 2019; Shekalaghe et al. 2014; Bastiaens et al. 2016; Lyke et al. 2015; Gómez-Pérez et al. 2015). Gómez-Pérez and colleagues demonstrated that IM injection of 75,000 PfSPZ and DVI injection of 3200 PfSPZ resulted in infection rates and pre patent periods comparable to the bite of five PfSPZ infected mosquitoes (Gómez-Pérez et al. 2015). Overall, the PfSPZ challenge studies have been reported to be safe and infectious in both malaria naïve and semi-immune malaria populations.2.2. Blood-Stage Challenge (Induced Blood-Stage Malaria (IBSM) Infection)
Although not as frequently used as the sporozoite mode of challenge, inoculation using blood-stage parasites can be done and was often used in the treatment of neurosyphilis patients in the early 1920s. IBSM results in blood-stage infection and is used to investigate blood stage as well as transmission-blocking vaccine candidates.
The first modern blood-stage CHMI was developed by Cheng and colleagues at the Queensland Institute for Medical Research and uses a cryopreserved stock of erythrocytes from parasitemic donors (Cheng et al. 1997). Two malaria naïve volunteers were infected with P. falciparum 3D7 parasites via mosquito bite challenge. 13–14 days later, when volunteers were microscopy positive and experiencing fever, 500 ml of blood was collected from each volunteer and cryopreserved. This master cell bank has been used as an inoculum in several IBSM challenge experiments investigating vaccine and drug efficacy (Cheng et al. 1997; Pombo et al. 2002; Duncan 2011; Lawrence et al. 2000). Post blood-stage challenge, monitoring of parasitemia is carried out by microscopy or highly sensitive qPCR assays, volunteers are then treated at a pre-defined parasite density (Cheng et al. 1997; Pombo et al. 2002).
The majority of challenge studies (sporozoites and blood-stage) are performed using P. falciparum as it is the most prevalent and pathogenic parasite (WHO. World Malaria report 2020). However, Plasmodium vivax has increasingly been recognized as a public health threat particularly in Asia and Latin America (Battle et al. 2019), as such challenge with P. vivax has also been achieved (Table 2) in both sporozoite and blood-stage models (McCarthy et al. 2013; Payne et al. 2017; Griffin 2016; Herrera et al. 2009; Arévalo-Herrera 2016). Currently, no modern-day blood-stage challenges have been carried out in Africa.
Table 2. Overview of induced blood-stage infection studies.

Study type
References

Plasmodium falciparum Pilot (Safety and infectivity) Vaccine efficacy

Cheng et al. (1997), Pombo et al. (2002), Sanderson et al. (2008), McCarthy et al. (2011)Duncan (2011), Lawrence et al. (2000), Sanderson et al. (2008), Payne et al. (2016), Minassian et al. (2021)

Plasmodium vivax Pilot (Safety and infectivity) Vaccine efficacy

McCarthy et al. (2013), Griffin (2016), Herrera et al. (2009), Collins et al. (2020), Herrera et al. (2011)Arévalo-Herrera (2016), Bennett et al. (2016), Lopez-perez (2016)

Plasmodium malariae Pilot (Safety and infectivity)

Woodford et al. (2020)

Open in a new tabOne of the main advantages of using the IBSM model is the ability to control the inoculum size; unlike the sporozoite challenge where the number of merozoites released from the liver varies as approximately 30,000 merozoites can be released into the bloodstream when a single infected hepatocyte ruptures (Bejon et al. 2005), the starting inoculum in a blood-stage challenge is much lower and tightly controlled which allows a longer period of observation of parasite growth increasing the number of time points at which to collect parasite data. This allows for the ability to calculate accurate parasite multiplication rates (PMR), the PMR is the per cycle fold-change in parasite numbers and is a measure of vaccine efficacy (VE) (Duncan and Draper 2012; Bejon et al. 2005). IBSM model is particularly useful in assessing VE of candidate blood-stage vaccines before introducing them to field studies. Blood stage vaccines are attractive as this is the stage that causes clinical symptoms (Miller et al. 2002). An effective blood-stage vaccine should demonstrate a measurable effect on PMR, especially during homologous challenges (Duncan 2011; Payne et al. 2016).
Recently, Minassian investigated the safety and efficacy of the vaccine candidate P. falciparum reticulocyte-binding protein homolog 5 (RH5) using blood-stage CHMI. Naïve volunteers were immunized with 3 doses of RH5 formulated in GlaxoSmithKline’s (GSK) adjuvant system AS01B followed by a blood-stage challenge with 1000 infected erythrocytes. The authors observed a reduction in parasite growth in the blood as well as immune mechanisms that could predict how well the vaccine performed (Minassian et al. 2021).
Blood stage challenge does have its limitations. Firstly, it circumvents the liver stage of parasite development bypassing immune responses induced by vaccination or natural infection at this stage (Sauerwein et al. 2011). This may underestimate VE as some vaccine candidates contain antigens that are shared between both liver and blood stages (Nahrendorf et al. 2015). The lack of liver stage development however, does have an advantage to the participants undergoing P. vivax challenge as there is no risk of hypnozoite formation or relapse (Baird et al. 2007; Auburn et al. 2021). Nonetheless, blood-stage challenges may be able to answer some of the questions that the sporozoite challenge cannot address, such as the immune mechanisms that confer protection. Secondly, there is considerable genetic heterogeneity of P. falciparum parasites circulating in the field. The ability to test different vaccine candidates against different strains is in both blood-stage and sporozoite challenge is crucial to vaccine development to determine candidates that offer strain transcending immunity (Cooper et al. 2019). To overcome this, several groups are working on expanding parasites strains for use in challenge studies (Stanisic et al. 2015).
Despite the challenges, the effort to eradicate malaria requires an understanding of all parasite stages and it has been demonstrated that NAI is largely against the blood-stage infection (Cohen et al. 1961) and clinical symptoms are observed during this stage. Therefore, blood-stage challenge still plays an important role in complementing the sporozoite challenge model when assessing potential vaccine candidates as well as identifying immune mechanisms of protection.

Plasmodium falciparum strains used in CHMI studies
In the field P. falciparum displays a wide genetic diversity, which is currently not represented by the available laboratory strains for CHMI. There are currently a limited number of defined P. falciparum strains used in CHMI: NF54 (an isolate of West African origin); 3D7 (a clone of NF54); 7G8 (a cloned line of the Brazilian IMTM22 isolate) (Burkot et al. 1984); NF135.C10 (a clone derived from a Cambodian isolate) (Teirlinck et al. 2013); and HMP02 (an isolate from Ghana), with the latter available only for a blood-stage challenge (Sheehy et al. 2013; Stanisic et al. 2018; Ponnudurai et al. 1981). When using NF54 or 3D7, the CHMI is considered homologous, since the parasite is identical to the challenge strain. When other strains are used, the CHMI is considered heterologous, and a potentially better predictor of efficacy under conditions of natural transmission, where mosquitoes harbor heterogeneous populations of P. falciparum (Richie et al. 2015). Whether these strains are representative of the antigenic diversity found in malaria endemic regions is unclear. The availability of aseptic, purified cryopreserved Pf sporozoites from Sanaria make NF54 the most widely used strain in CHMI studies. Moderate cross reactive protection against heterologous challenge and natural exposure has been observed in both naïve adults and semi-immune individuals suggesting that protection is partially strain specific (Schats et al. 2015; Walk et al. 2017; Sissoko et al. 2017; Kapulu et al. 2020; Lyke et al. 2021; Moser et al. 2020; Ishizuka et al. 2016).
Interpretation of data from CHMI studies and in turn vaccine efficacy trials involving challenge need to take into account whether the challenge was homologous or heterologous and whether the strains used are representative of the parasite’s antigenic diversity in that region (Stanisic et al. 2018).3. Establishment of CHMI in Africa
One of the first human challenge studies in Africa was carried out by Alison and colleagues in 1954, where they investigated the protective effect of sickle cell trait against malaria infection. They looked at two groups of adult males; 15 with sickle cell trait and 15 without, the researchers inoculated the volunteers with either blood containing a large number of trophozoites or by biting with heavily infected Anopheles gambiae. They demonstrated an infection with P. falciparum was established in 14 out of 15 without the sickle-cell trait, compared to only 2 out of 15 in the sickle cell group demonstrating the protective nature of sickle cell trait in malaria (Allison 1954). Another study conducted in Liberia inoculated semi-immune adults with P. falciparum via infected A. gambiae mosquitoes (Bray et al. 1962). However, the majority of the ‘modern’ CHMI studies have since been conducted in malaria-naïve populations (Stanisic et al. 2018) which eliminates the potential confounder of prior exposure.
Conducting CHMI studies in malaria-endemic areas offers a huge advantage over malaria naïve individuals, firstly it provides an opportunity to understand the effect of naturally acquired immunity and subsequently, the effect it has on a vaccine and/or drug efficacy as well as assessing vaccine safety and efficacy. Secondly, the trial population matches the actual target population having the same genetic background, and lastly, it offers the opportunity to study the effects of co-infections which can influence vaccine and immune responses (Nouatin et al. 2021). To date, P. falciparum CHMI studies have been conducted in 6 African countries summarized in Tables 1 and 2.
Modern day CHMI studies in Africa
The first modern CHMI study in Africa using cryopreserved PfSPZ was in 2012 (Shekalaghe et al. 2014). The study was conducted in 30 healthy adult males living in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania. The volunteers in this study were considered semi-immune as they had not had a clinical episode of malaria for five years, however, had a positive P. falciparum lysate serology before CHMI, indicating a previous exposure. Volunteers were divided into two dose groups with one group receiving 10,000 PfSPZ ID and the other 25,000 PfSPZ ID. The study demonstrated PfSPZ challenge was safe, well-tolerated, and infectious in adults with a previous history of malaria exposure with 21 out of 24 volunteers being parasite positive by day 21. Malaria diagnosis was carried out by thick blood smear with qPCR performed retrospectively. Further safety and infectivity studies have been carried out using Sanaria PfSPZ in adults with varying degrees of malaria exposure in Kenya (Kapulu et al. 2020, 2018; Hodgson et al. 2014), Gabon (Lell et al. 2018; Dejon-Agobe et al. 2019), Gambia (Achan 2019), Equatorial Guinea (Jongo et al. 2021) and all studies have demonstrated CHMI to be safe and infectious in these populations despite the route of administration (summarized in Table 3). Administration of sporozoites was done by ID in Tanzanian trial; IM in the Kenyan study and DVI in the Gabon, Gambia, and Equatorial Guinea studies (Table 4).

Open in a new tabCHMI studies to understand naturally acquired immunity and infectivity
Although slow to develop, naturally acquired immunity (NAI) to malaria does exist. Adults who have grown up in an endemic area and are exposed to repeated P. falciparum infections develop immunity, first to severe malaria and then to clinical disease (Doolan et al. 2009). NAI is directed mainly against the asexual parasite cycle in the blood and is key to the rational development and deployment of vaccines. NAI to malaria involves both antibody-mediated and cell-mediated immunity and can be divided broadly into 3 stages (i) pre-erythrocytic (ii) erythrocytic and (iii) anti-gametocyte immunity.
Besides being a powerful tool to study vaccine and drug efficacy, CHMI models offer a way to understand the immune mechanism involved in naturally acquired protective immunity and offer a perfect opportunity to study the effect and the mechanisms of NAI on parasite growth kinetics in a well-controlled setting. In addition, challenge models may aid in the possible identification of immunological correlates of protection. Identifying and establishing correlates of protection and/or immunological substitute endpoints can accelerate vaccine development and contribute to new vaccine approaches and strategies (Stanisic and McCall 2021). Studies have been carried out looking at malaria specific immune responses provoked by parasite exposure during CHMI in naïve individuals recently reviewed by Yap and colleagues. They demonstrated CHMI volunteers are either fast/inflammatory responders or slow/antibody mediated responders (Yap et al. 2020).
When using CHMI models in endemic areas, assessment of vaccine or drug efficacy may be complicated by NAI, although arguably given the existence of NAI in the target populations this is an equally important assessment. Asymptomatic infections present prior to CHMI may also complicate assessments (Cooper et al. 2019), although this can be mitigated by screening for infection and using anti-malarial drugs with short half-life before conducting challenge. Studies looking at the effect of natural immunity using the CHMI model in endemic areas are summarized in Table 5.

        Abstract

      Self-awareness, the ability to take oneself as the object of awareness, has been an enigma for our species, with different answers to this question being provided by religion, philosophy, and, more recently, science. The current review aims to discuss the neurocognitive mechanisms underlying self-awareness. The multidimensional nature of self-awareness will be explored, suggesting how it can be thought of as an emergent property observed in different cognitive complexity levels, within a predictive coding approach. A presentation of alterations of self-awareness in neuropsychiatric conditions will ground a discussion on alternative frameworks to understand this phenomenon, in health and psychopathology, with future research directions being indicated to fill current gaps in the literature. This article is categorized under: Philosophy > Consciousness Psychology > Brain Function and Dysfunction Neuroscience > Cognition.

          Keywords:

      agency; interoception; metacognition; neuropsychiatric conditions; self-awareness.

entry.content:
<skill name="pdf">
# PDF Processing Guide

## Overview

This guide covers essential PDF processing operations using Python libraries and command-line tools. For advanced features, JavaScript libraries, and detailed examples, see reference.md. If you need to fill out a PDF form, read forms.md and follow its instructions.

## Quick Start

```python
from pypdf import PdfReader, PdfWriter

# Read a PDF
reader = PdfReader("document.pdf")
print(f"Pages: {len(reader.pages)}")

# Extract text
text = ""
for page in reader.pages:
    text += page.extract_text()
```

## Python Libraries

### pypdf - Basic Operations

#### Merge PDFs
```python
from pypdf import PdfWriter, PdfReader

writer = PdfWriter()
for pdf_file in ["doc1.pdf", "doc2.pdf", "doc3.pdf"]:
    reader = PdfReader(pdf_file)
    for page in reader.pages:
        writer.add_page(page)

with open("merged.pdf", "wb") as output:
    writer.write(output)
```

#### Split PDF
```python
reader = PdfReader("input.pdf")
for i, page in enumerate(reader.pages):
    writer = PdfWriter()
    writer.add_page(page)
    with open(f"page_{i+1}.pdf", "wb") as output:
        writer.write(output)
```

#### Extract Metadata
```python
reader = PdfReader("document.pdf")
meta = reader.metadata
print(f"Title: {meta.title}")
print(f"Author: {meta.author}")
print(f"Subject: {meta.subject}")
print(f"Creator: {meta.creator}")
```

#### Rotate Pages
```python
reader = PdfReader("input.pdf")
writer = PdfWriter()

page = reader.pages[0]
page.rotate(90)  # Rotate 90 degrees clockwise
writer.add_page(page)

with open("rotated.pdf", "wb") as output:
    writer.write(output)
```

### pdfplumber - Text and Table Extraction

#### Extract Text with Layout
```python
import pdfplumber

with pdfplumber.open("document.pdf") as pdf:
    for page in pdf.pages:
        text = page.extract_text()
        print(text)
```

#### Extract Tables
```python
with pdfplumber.open("document.pdf") as pdf:
    for i, page in enumerate(pdf.pages):
        tables = page.extract_tables()
        for j, table in enumerate(tables):
            print(f"Table {j+1} on page {i+1}:")
            for row in table:
                print(row)
```

#### Advanced Table Extraction
```python
import pandas as pd

with pdfplumber.open("document.pdf") as pdf:
    all_tables = []
    for page in pdf.pages:
        tables = page.extract_tables()
        for table in tables:
            if table:  # Check if table is not empty
                df = pd.DataFrame(table[1:], columns=table[0])
                all_tables.append(df)

# Combine all tables
if all_tables:
    combined_df = pd.concat(all_tables, ignore_index=True)
    combined_df.to_excel("extracted_tables.xlsx", index=False)
```

### reportlab - Create PDFs

#### Basic PDF Creation
```python
from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import letter
from reportlab.pdfgen import canvas

c = canvas.Canvas("hello.pdf", pagesize=letter)
width, height = letter

# Add text
c.drawString(100, height - 100, "Hello World!")
c.drawString(100, height - 120, "This is a PDF created with reportlab")

# Add a line
c.line(100, height - 140, 400, height - 140)

# Save
c.save()
```

#### Create PDF with Multiple Pages
```python
from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import letter
from reportlab.platypus import SimpleDocTemplate, Paragraph, Spacer, PageBreak
from reportlab.lib.styles import getSampleStyleSheet

doc = SimpleDocTemplate("report.pdf", pagesize=letter)
styles = getSampleStyleSheet()
story = []

# Add content
title = Paragraph("Report Title", styles['Title'])
story.append(title)
story.append(Spacer(1, 12))

body = Paragraph("This is the body of the report. " * 20, styles['Normal'])
story.append(body)
story.append(PageBreak())

# Page 2
story.append(Paragraph("Page 2", styles['Heading1']))
story.append(Paragraph("Content for page 2", styles['Normal']))

# Build PDF
doc.build(story)
```

## Command-Line Tools

### pdftotext (poppler-utils)
```bash
# Extract text
pdftotext input.pdf output.txt

# Extract text preserving layout
pdftotext -layout input.pdf output.txt

# Extract specific pages
pdftotext -f 1 -l 5 input.pdf output.txt  # Pages 1-5
```

### qpdf
```bash
# Merge PDFs
qpdf --empty --pages file1.pdf file2.pdf -- merged.pdf

# Split pages
qpdf input.pdf --pages . 1-5 -- pages1-5.pdf
qpdf input.pdf --pages . 6-10 -- pages6-10.pdf

# Rotate pages
qpdf input.pdf output.pdf --rotate=+90:1  # Rotate page 1 by 90 degrees

# Remove password
qpdf --password=mypassword --decrypt encrypted.pdf decrypted.pdf
```

### pdftk (if available)
```bash
# Merge
pdftk file1.pdf file2.pdf cat output merged.pdf

# Split
pdftk input.pdf burst

14→The point at which these self-monitoring abilities lead to subjective awareness remains discussed. One perspec-
15→tive to understand self-awareness would be to consider it as an example of an emergent property. According to this
16→view, interactions at a lower level lead to new properties that are greater than the sum of their parts, and to which
17→a specific identity must be recognized (Artime & De Domenico, 2022; McClelland et al., 2010). Within this perspec-
18→tive, implicit sources of information would interact and be integrated into explicit, aware, self-models. The notion of
19→emergence not only prevents reifying self-awareness, indicating it is a process, rather than a thing in our brains, but
20→also avoids the idea of an infinite regress in relation to selfhood. If more complex levels are but the result of the
21→interaction of lower, less complex levels, the capacities at each level are neither replicated above or below. With
22→decreasing levels of cognitive complexity, subjective experience is lost at some point of the reduction
23→(Dennett, 2014).

27→in functional neurological disorders (FND). In one study, people with functional seizures demonstrated lower intero-

29→measures,referredtoastheinteroceptivetraitpredictionerror,wasastrongpredictorofdissociativesymptoms(Koreki

31→People with schizophrenia also demonstrate evidence of reduced interoceptive accuracy (Monti et al., 2021). For
32→example,inastudyof23peoplewithschizophrenia,interoceptiveaccuracymeasuredusingaheartbeatperceptiontask
33→was significantly lower than controls (Ardizzi et al., 2016), and a recent transdiagnostic study also found evidence for
34→pervasive interoceptive deficits in schizophrenia (Critchley et al., 2019). Impairments in interoception are associated
35→with abnormalities in a network of brain regions including the insular cortex (Bonaz et al., 2021). A recent
36→meta-analysis of neuroimaging studies including a range of interoceptive tasks and neuropsychiatric conditions found
37→evidence of a common alteration in dorsal mid-insula activation, which may suggest a common role of interoceptive
38→dysfunctionacrossseveralmentalhealthdisorders(Nordetal.,2021).
39→3.2 | Proprioception and body ownership

 6→beenshowntobecompoundedbycomorbidbipolaraffectivedisorder(Bayesetal.,2016).Emotionalregulation isalso
 7→found to be impaired in individuals with a solitary diagnosis of bipolar affective disorder, albeit to a lesserdegree than

 9→Other psychiatric disorders in which emotional regulation has also been shown to be adversely affected
10→include autism spectrum disorder (ASD) (Joshi et al., 2018), substance misuse disorder (Parolin et al., 2017), and
11→attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (El Archi et al., 2023). Different disorders may affect different mechanisms
12→of emotional regulation. For example, it has been shown that in social anxiety there is an overreliance in expres-
13→sive suppression of emotions, while in major depressive disorder cognitive reappraisal is underutilized (Dryman &
14→Heimberg, 2018).
15→3.6 | Autobiographical memory
16→Acentral component ofself-awareness isautobiographicalmemory. Deficits inautobiographical memory areseenina
17→rangeofneuropsychiatricconditionsandareacharacteristicfeatureofmostformsofdementia.InAlzheimer'sdisease
18→(AD), deficits in episodic and semantic memory contribute to impaired autobiographical self-awareness (Mograbi
19→etal.,2021).Deficits inautonoetic consciousness, thesubjective conscious experience that accompanies episodicmem-
20→oryretrievalandinvolves“mentaltimetravel”backtotheevent,autobiographicalknowledgeandasenseofcontinuity
21→of the self occurs at the mild–moderate stages of AD (Huntley et al., 2021). People with AD may also be impaired in
22→updating and integrating autobiographical information into their narrative sense of self, leading to a lack of self-
23→awarenessofcurrentdeficits(Lenzonietal.,2020).
24→Other common neuropsychiatric conditions also demonstrate alterations in autobiographical memory. Autobio-
25→graphicaldysfunctionmaybebothacauseandconsequenceofdepression(Talarowskaetal.,2016),withautobiograph-
26→ical memory recall identified as more generalized and less specific in recurrent depressive disorders (Hamlat
27→etal.,2014;Hermans etal.,2008).People withdepressionarelessabletoaccessspecific autobiographicalmemories in
28→response to positive and neutral stimuli than controls (Young et al., 2012) or recall and reconstruct details of events,
29→particularly those that were positive and may not easily integrate with a negative sense of self (Lemogne et al., 2006).
30→Similarly, depressed bipolar patients report memories with fewer perceptual and time integration details than those in
31→euthymia ormania(Da Silvaet al.,2021). Over-general recall ofautobiographicalmemory also occurs inpatients with
32→schizophrenia,withlessretrievalofspecificmemories,whichisassociatedwithpeoplewithschizophreniaexperiencing
33→abnormalitiesintheirconceptofself(McLeodetal.,2006;Zhangetal.,2019).
34→4 | ALTERNATIVE FRAMEWORKS AND FUTURE RESEARCH DIRECTIONS
35→Theevidence reviewedhere indicates both thepervasivenessand diversity ofself-awareness alterations inclinical con-
36→ditions. The pattern of changes, within and across clinical groups, may help in developing cognitive frameworks to
37→understandself-awareness.HereaPCapproachmaybeparticularlyhelpfultomakesenseofthedata.
38→An initial perspective is to consider that, in some conditions, difficulties may arise in how higher-order processes
39→constrain incoming bottom-upinformation. Forinstance, indepressionand anxiety, changes inbeliefs mayaffectallo-
40→cation of attention and the interpretation of interoceptive signals. This may happen even across self-awareness pro-
41→cesses. For instance, it is possible that emotional regulation beliefs impact directly on interoceptive processing
42→(Critchley&Garfinkel,2017).
43→This model, with alterations in higher-order constructs leading to changes in lower-level processes, may be helpful
44→in understanding conditions such as OCD, in which beliefs and thoughts about thoughts are central (Myers &
45→Wells,2005).Itispossible,however,thatthepriorsthemselvesareeitherreinforcedorformedonthebasisofdistorted
46→information.Thiswouldleadtotwodifferentperspectives:(1)concomitantchangesinlow-andhigher-orderprocesses,
47→with an altered interpretation of signals reinforcing beliefs and beliefs constraining signals, in a cycle; (2) a primarily
48→bottom-uporiginfordifficulties,withbeliefsbeingformedfromdisruptedincominginformation.Stillintheexampleof

 1→MOGRABIETAL. 9of15
 2→OCD,itispossiblethatlimbichyperactivationanddisruptedemotionalprocessingleadtothealteredthinkingpatterns
 3→ofthecondition(Simonetal.,2014;Thorsenetal.,2018).
 4→In conditions such as schizophrenia, problems seem to appear across self-awareness processes, for instance with
 5→increasedinteroceptivesensitivity,poor bodilyintegrationand decreasedagency,but alsometacognitivedistortions. In
 6→this case, it is harder to suggest a straightforward chain of causality. These findings may suggest either a concomitant
 7→origin or a spurious correlation; in the latter, other changes, central to the condition, would lead to the profile of self-
 8→awarenessalterations,whichcaneitherbeconsideredanepiphenomenonorareinforcingfactorinthesubjectiveexpe-
 9→rienceofpatients.
10→The expansion of a transdiagnostic approach to study self-awareness may deliver results with relevant clinical and
11→theoretical implications. For instance, exploring how different self-awareness processes are affected across disorders
12→mayrevealshareddiagnosticcharacteristics,goingbeyondacategoricalapproach.Thisisconsistentwithrecentefforts
13→to describe psychopathology in terms of dimensional features (Cuthbert, 2014), and may indicate relevant targets for
14→intervention, both forpharmacological and psychosocial treatments. Although there is already some existing evidence,
15→such as the studies reviewed in the previous section, further data collection may assist in determining mechanisms
16→behindself-awarenessinhealthandillness.
17→Conversely, exploring different self-awareness processes within single clinical conditions may not only provide
18→importantresourcesforinterventions,butalsoshedlightintothecognitivemechanismsofself-awareness.Forexample,
19→investigating how bottom-up and top-down processes from different self-awareness subdomains interact, may indicate
20→if there is a hierarchical relationship between these subdomains, with some capacities being “nested” in others
21→(Feinberg, 2011). Exploring a variety of self-awareness processes within the same condition may also help clarify the
22→interactionbetweenself-awarenesslevelsandthemechanismsleadingtotheemergenceofsubjectiveexperience.
23→For this to happen, methodological advances in how to quantify self-awareness are needed. Different approaches
24→have been used depending on the specific self-awareness process, including questionnaires (e.g., MAIA; Mehling
25→etal.,2012),behavioraltests(e.g.,agency,Imaizumi&Tanno, 2019)andphysiologicalmeasures (e.g.,measurementof
26→event-related potentials for error monitoring, Lenzoni et al., 2021; resting-state magnetic resonance imaging; Ham et
27→al.,2013),butcomparativelyfewerstudies have integrated methods acrossthesemeasurement levels.Forexample, the
28→development of “self-awareness test batteries,” including questionnaires and behavioral measures, which can be
29→coupledwithphysiologicaldata,mayelucidatetherelationshipbetweenself-awarenesslevelsandprocesses.Privileging
30→ecological validity is another important focus for the development of new measurements, for instance with the use of
31→virtual reality, which can provide immersive environments maintaining experimental control. Additionally, the devel-
32→opment of new methods that can tap into the level of self-awareness of people with disorders of consciousness
33→(e.g., persistent vegetative state, coma) is particularly relevant from an ethical point of view, for instance impacting on
34→end-of-lifedecision-making.
35→Elucidating the neurochemical basis of self-awareness may have direct implications, not only for our understanding
36→ofthisphenomenon,butalsofordiagnosisandtreatmentofneuropsychiatricconditions.Anapproachwouldbetoiden-
37→tifywhichneurotransmittersareimplicatedinalterationsofself-awareness.Forexample,theroleofdopamineinagency
38→hasbeensuggested,withhigheraccessibilityofdopaminepotentiallyincreasingsenseofagency(Render&Jansen,2019).
39→Suchfindingswouldindicatetargetsforintervention,newpotentialtherapiesandhighlightclinicalgroupswhoaremore
40→vulnerable to these changes. Another avenue of research would be to explore substances with traditional use that more
41→recentlyhavebeenincorporatedintomainstreamtreatments.Forexample,psilocybinhasbeenknowntoleadtochanges
42→in the sense of self, with users typically reporting “ego-dissolution” (a reduction of self-reference and a disintegration in
43→the boundaries between the self and the world), in both negative and positive experiences with psilocybin (Bienemann
44→et al., 2020; 2023). Considering the boom in research on psychedelics, investigating self-awareness may help bringing
45→someofthesecompoundsintoclinicalpractice,alsoprovidingresourcesforacognitiveneuroscienceofself-awareness.
46→Finally, exploring self-awareness across species may be crucial to understand the origins of this phenomenon
47→and its evolutionary pathways. A comparative approach may indicate the extent to which levels of self-awareness
48→are present in other species, as well as the particularities of certain processes in specific clades (Lage et al., 2022).
49→Expanding our knowledge about the level of self-awareness of other species may have an important impact in our
50→relationship with them. This evolutionary perspective may also shed light into another frontier of human knowl-
51→edge, namely the capacity of devices to become self-aware. Various attempts have been made to explore this issue
52→(Chella et al., 2019; McCarthy, 2002; Scheutz, 2014), for example, investigating the extent to which inner speech
53→may be linked to self-awareness in robots (Chella et al., 2020). This could help revealing if inner speech is suffi-
54→cient for the development of self-awareness (or, rather, which type of self-awareness may emerge from such an

 1→10of15 MOGRABIETAL.
 2→attempt), while an evolutionary approach may indicate whether this construct is necessary or not. By exploring
 3→self-awareness in devices and increasing our understanding of the biological conditions in which self-awareness
 4→emerges, we will be more equipped to develop models and simulations of the phenomenon, also increasing our
 5→knowledge about the relevance of the material support grounding self-awareness, as well as its potential to be
 6→instantiated elsewhere.
 7→5 | CONCLUSIONS
 8→Thesearchforself-awarenesshasbeenajourney forourspecies,with answerstothisquestion being traditionallypro-
 9→vided by religion and philosophy. A cognitive neuroscience of self-awareness has only been emerging in the past few
10→decades, but growing evidence has been shedding light into the phenomenon, not least in the case of neuropsychiatric
11→conditions, as reviewed here. It is expected that the expansion of this field will have implications for theoretical, clini-
12→cal,andethicalperspectives.
13→Oneimportantlimitationofthecurrentreviewreferstothelackofevidencefromacrossconditions,withfewstudies
14→directly comparing different disorders. Although such studies are indeed scarce, we hope that the current work will
15→motivate researchers to pursue this further, not least because this transdiagnostic approach may also be relevant clini-
16→cally. Additionally, some may argue against the attempt to bring processes as diverse as interoception and metacogni-
17→tionundertheumbrellaofself-awareness.Indeed,partofthescientificendeavorisgroundedinbreakinguprealityinto
18→smaller components, in order to better understand its mechanisms, then reassembling its pieces. The approach indi-
19→cated here does not replace the need for the examination of individual self-awareness processes, but suggests that
20→exploringthesetogethermayrevealsharedmechanismsandinteractionsthatcouldbehardertoidentifyinisolation.
21→Our cognitive faculties have evolved with the sole purpose of keeping us alive, helping us to navigate the environ-
22→ment and identify internal demands and their level of satisfaction. Within this perspective, cognition is self-serving by
23→definition. By exploring the mechanisms of self-awareness, a finer understanding of brain function and cognitive
24→processingmaybeachieved,helpingustoanswertheperennialquestionofwhoweare,bothfromamechanisticanda
25→subjectiveperspective.

## Mograbi, Hall, Arantes & Huntley (2023/2024)

> **原文**：The cognitive neuroscience of self-awareness: Current framework, clinical implications, and future research directions
>
> **出处**：WIREs Cognitive Science, 2024, 15(2): e1670 | DOI: 10.1002/wcs.1670
>
> **作者**：Daniel C. Mograbi (PUC-Rio / King's College London), Simon Hall (Camden & Islington NHS), Beatriz Arantes (PUC-Rio), Jonathan Huntley (UCL / Exeter)
>
> **性质**：综述（Advanced Review），非实证研究

## 问题

你拍脑门问自己：「自我意识是什么？」绝大多数人会回答：「我知道我是我。」你追问：「这发生在脑的什么地方？」经典回答指向前额叶——那个最「人类」的皮层，负责元认知、自我参照、反思。Dehaene、Damasio、Northoff 各有各的候选脑区。但看了一圈临床数据你会感到不安：精神分裂症的自我意识问题是全面的——身体感、自主感、元认知、自传体记忆全乱了。自闭症的内感受异常和情绪调节异常并存。抑郁症的不止是自我评价降低，自传体记忆也在塌——只记得负面的、笼统的。边缘型人格障碍的情绪调节彻底崩。阿尔茨海默症的自传体记忆逐片剥落。

这些病症彼此的损伤模式没有显示出任何单一脑区的垄断。如果是前额叶坏了→自我意识没了，那应该有一组前额叶损伤患者表现出整齐的统一症状。但临床现实不是这样——不同病变、不同病症损伤的是自我意识的不同侧面，相邻而独立。

所以我们可能问错了问题。不问「自我意识在哪个脑区」，问「自我意识是哪几个过程在什么框架下交互涌现的」。Mograbi 团队 2023/2024 年这篇综述，就是来回答这个问题的。

他们要把六个自我意识过程放进一个统一的认知架构——预测编码（Predictive Coding）——然后拿临床神经精神疾病当验证：如果这个架构是对的，那么不同病症应该损伤不同过程，而且损伤模式应该在预测编码框架里可解释。

---

## 翻译

### 一、自我意识不是一个东西，是六个过程的交互

文章梳理了六种自我意识过程，每种有不同的认知对象和神经底物。

#### 1. 内感受（Interoception）

**定义**：感知身体生理状态——心跳、呼吸、胃的饱胀、膀胱的充盈。「我饿了」「我心慌」「我呼吸急促」的神经基础。

**这不是一个小众感觉**——它是自我表征最底层的材料。维持内稳态的内隐反射（心率、血压、呼吸节律的自动调节）是内感受的隐性版本；当你意识到「心跳很快、手心出汗」，那是内感受的显性版本。

关键区分（Garfinkel et al., 2015）：内感受精度（客观检测到心跳的准确性）、内感受敏感度（你主观认为自己多擅长感知身体）、内感受觉察（前两者的匹配度——你对自己内感受能力的自知之明）。三者在不同精神病症里损伤模式不同。

**神经基础**：岛叶皮层是核心枢纽——特别是背侧中岛叶。经颅诊断学元分析（Nord et al., 2021）发现跨多种精神疾病的共同内感受异常汇聚在背侧中岛叶的激活异常上。

#### 2. 身体所有权与本体感觉（Proprioception & Body Ownership）

**定义**：知道自己身体各部分的相对位置（本体感觉），以及体验这个身体属于自己（身体所有权）。「我的手是我的一部分」不是不言自明的天赋——它是多感官整合的产物。

**证据**：橡胶手错觉（Rubber Hand Illusion）。同步触摸真手和假手→大脑把假手纳入身体模型。精神分裂症患者在 RHI 里显示异常的身体所有权感，且异常程度与幻觉、妄想、被动症状、快感缺失严重度相关。PTSD 和人格解体障碍患者出现部分身体被体验为「非我」的现象。

**神经基础**：岛叶再次出现。人格解体障碍的 fMRI 研究中岛叶激活异常（Medford et al., 2016）。多感官整合是一套皮层-皮层下回路：顶叶（空间整合）、前运动皮层（身体图式更新）、岛叶（身体的「这属于我」属性标记）、颞顶交界（自我/非我区分）。

#### 3. 自主感（Agency）

**定义**：体验自己是行动的发起者。「是我动了手指」vs「手指自己动了」。经典理论依赖**传出副本**（efference copy）——发出运动指令时同时发出一份副本用于预测行动后果。预测与感觉反馈匹配→自主感；不匹配→「这不是我干的」。

**破坏后的表现**：精神分裂症的被动体验（「我的手被外力控制了」）和思维插入（「这些想法不是我的」）。Renes et al. (2013) 的精巧实验：精神分裂症患者在显性控制条件下体验自主感正常，但在 17ms 阈下启动的隐性控制条件下自主感显著缺失。自主感的隐性通路先断了——这比显性通路更基础。

反向：右半球损伤的疾病失认偏瘫患者把自己瘫侧肢体的运动归因为自己的（增加的自主体验）。过度自主体验→病理性内疚——「这一切都是我的错」。

**神经基础**：多巴胺系统与自主感密切相关——更高的多巴胺可用性可能增加自主体验（Render & Jansen, 2019）。传出副本涉及前 SMA（辅助运动区前部）→ 顶叶比较器回路。

#### 4. 元认知（Metacognition）

**定义**：对自己的认知进行监测、知识和调节。「我有多确定这个答案？」「我擅长这件事吗？」「我需要放慢一点。」比内感受和自主感高一阶——它有递归性（思考思考）。

**临床损伤**：精神分裂症的 MAS（元认知评估量表）三域都受影响——理解自己的心智（自我归因缺陷、对信念的不恰当确信→妄想、缺乏自知力）、理解他人的心智（推断他人情绪准确性下降→社交功能差）、掌控（调节心理状态的策略贫乏→社交图式贫乏→恶性循环）。

强迫症的元认知缺陷特别典型：记忆信心低→检查行为重复（不管是因还是果，都在维持循环）；「思维可控性」的元信念是 OCD 的核心。

**神经基础**：前额叶（特别是 DLPFC 和内侧前额叶）是元认知的核心。这与内感受/身体所有权/自主感不同——那些依赖岛叶、顶叶和皮层下结构。元认知是前额叶的专属领地。

#### 5. 情绪调节（Emotional Regulation）

**定义**：对情绪过程的思考、策略和反应。Gross (2002) 的经典模型分两类——**前因聚焦**（选择情境→修正情境→注意分配→认知重评）和**反应相关**（反应抑制、攻击、物质滥用）。

**临床损伤**：边缘型人格障碍的标志——情绪调节崩解导致关系破坏和自杀危险。即使在 40-59 岁组与 18-25 岁组同样严重（Martino et al., 2020）——这是一个跨生命周期的稳定损伤。不同疾病损伤不同调节策略：社交焦虑过度依赖表达抑制，重性抑郁缺乏认知重评（Dryman & Heimberg, 2018）。

**神经基础**：前额叶（DLPFC、VMPFC）→ 杏仁核、岛叶的自上而下调控。情绪调节同时涉及前额叶和皮层下结构的交互——这是高阶自我意识借由预测编码约束低阶内感受信号的一个直接例子。

#### 6. 自传体记忆（Autobiographical Memory）

**定义**：回忆关于自己个人历史的记忆。分两类——事件记忆（「大二那年冬天我跟朋友……」）和个人语义（「我在 X 大学读书」）。连续谱，大多数记忆在这两极之间。

**临床损伤**：阿尔茨海默症——事件记忆和个人语义的丧失导致自传体自我（autobiographical self）渐渐瓦解。中度 AD 开始失去自主意识（autonoetic consciousness）——那个「心智时间旅行」回到过去的自我体验。更重要的是，AD 患者无法将新信息整合进自我叙事→对当前缺陷缺乏自知力（Lenzoni et al., 2020）。

复发性抑郁的自传体记忆是笼统的、过度概括化的——对正面和中性刺激难以提取具体回忆（Young et al., 2012），对负面记忆则过度聚焦。这与负面自我信念相互强化——自传体记忆既是抑郁的因（笼统的记忆无法提供正面自我参照的证据）也是果（负面自我概念过滤记忆提取）。

精神分裂症的自传体记忆同样是过度概括化的，伴随自我概念的异常。

**神经基础**：海马（事件记忆编码/提取）、前额叶（检索策略和监控）、PCC/楔前叶（自我参照加工和自传体检索的汇聚点）、角回（个人语义整合）。这与 DMN 高度重叠。

---

### 二、六个过程如何统一：预测编码框架

Mograbi 团队的**核心架构选择**是把所有六个过程放进**预测编码（Predictive Coding, PC）**。这不是一个新理论——它的根在 Helmholtz 的「无意识推理」概念，在 Friston 的自由能原理那里得到了数学形式化。但把 PC 系统地应用到自我意识的所有六个子过程、然后用临床数据统一验证——这是新的。

#### PC 怎么讲自我意识？

> 大脑是一个**推理机器**。它持续生成自上而下的预测（priors），与自下而上的感觉信息比较。不匹配 = 预测误差。最小化预测误差有两个方向：**修正信念**（更新内部模型）或**改变输入**（通过行动改变世界）。

应用到六个过程：

| 过程 | 自上而下的预测 | 自下而上的感觉输入 | 预测误差的含义 |
|------|:-------------|:-----------------|:-------------|
| **内感受** | 对身体生理状态的信念 | 内脏传入信号 | 修正内感受信念或自主神经调节 |
| **身体所有权** | 身体图式模板 | 多感官（视觉、触觉、本体感觉） | 更新身体模型（如橡胶手错觉） |
| **自主感** | 传出副本对行动后果的预测 | 实际感觉反馈 | 匹配→我做的；不匹配→不是我 |
| **元认知** | 对自己能力的信念 | 任务表现的客观反馈 | 更新元认知信念（信心校准） |
| **情绪调节** | 对情绪状态的期望和调节策略 | 生理唤起和情感信号 | 重新评估情境或调整调节策略 |
| **自传体记忆** | 自我叙事框架 | 具体回忆细节 | 整合或过滤记忆以维持一致性 |

#### ISAM：整合自我意识模型

文章的 **Figure 1** 是 ISAM（Integrated Self-Awareness Model）——这是这篇文章的方法论支柱。核心逻辑：

1. **身体自我意识**（内感受 → 身体所有权 → 自主感）形成一个嵌套层级。内感受的信号也馈入身体所有权和自主感——心跳的变化影响你能不能分辨这是你的手动了。
2. **高阶自我意识**（元认知、情绪调节、自传体记忆）受内隐自下而上信号影响，但它们表达为显性的**叙事自我表征**——它们之间不一定是层级关系，而是平行交互的。
3. 身体自我意识和高阶自我表征在**特定过程的层面**和**更高整合层面**两个层次上交互→涌现出一个整合的自我意识体验。

**关键**：自我意识不是任何单一过程的产物，也不是任何单一脑区的活动。它是六个过程在预测编码框架下的动态交互中涌现的——自下而上的信息和自上而下的先验在每层都在比较和协商，整体形成「我是我」的体验。

#### 涌现而非实体

文章反复强调一个点：自我意识是**涌现属性（emergent property）**——低层交互产生高层属性，这些属性不可还原为低层组分的简单相加。

这不只是哲学点缀。它意味着：
- 自我意识不是可定位的「东西」——它是过程，不是物件。
- 随着认知复杂度降低（往下拆），主观体验在某个还原层次**消失**（Dennett, 2014）。
- 自我意识是分级现象，不是二元的——有不同程度的自我意识，从内隐的稳态监控一直到递归的元认知。

这解释了为什么**最低等的生物也能自我监控**（为了维持内稳态），但没有高层自我意识。演化把低层的自监控机制越叠越复杂，最终涌现出人类级别的自反思维。

---

### 三、临床验证：每个病症损伤不同的过程

文章第三部分是临床数据的巡礼——以表 1 为总览：

| 过程 | 定义 | 受影响的临床条件 |
|------|------|:--------------|
| 内感受 | 感知全身的生理状态 | 述情障碍、焦虑障碍、ASD、抑郁、功能性神经障碍、精神分裂症、进食障碍 |
| 本体感觉/身体所有权 | 身体位置图和身体归属感 | 功能性运动障碍、精神分裂症、躯体异己感、PTSD、人格解体障碍 |
| 自主感 | 体验自己产生行动 | 精神分裂症、异己手综合征、偏瘫疾病失认 |
| 元认知 | 对认知的监测、知识和调节 | 痴呆、OCD、PTSD、精神病 |
| 情绪调节 | 对情绪的监测和调节 | 边缘型人格障碍、物质滥用障碍、双相情感障碍、ASD、ADHD |
| 自传体记忆 | 自我信息的记录（事件和个人知识） | 痴呆、解离性遗忘、PTSD、抑郁、精神分裂症 |

**关键发现**：
- 没有一个脑区或过程是「自我意识的总开关」——不同疾病打击不同过程，且经常同时打击多个过程，但打击模式各有特征。
- 精神分裂症是最全面的打击——六个过程几乎全有异常。这解释了这个病症特殊的主观体验：「我不再是我的思想和行动的主人」。
- 岛叶背侧中部是一个跨诊断的会聚点——多个精神疾病的内感受异常都在这里找到共同的神经信号（Nord et al., 2021）。

#### PC 框架如何解释不同疾病的损伤模式？

文章提出三种可能性，不同疾病可能对应不同的模式：

1. **自上而下主导**：先验/信念出错→错误地约束自下而上信号。抑郁症和焦虑症最符合——负面的自我信念过度解释和过滤内感受信号（心跳=恐慌→灾难化→恐慌发作）。
2. **自下而上主导**：传入信号异常→形成扭曲的信念。可能部分适用于 OCD——边缘系统过度激活→情绪加工扭曲→形成「思维必须被控制」的元信念。
3. **双向循环**：高层和低层同时或相互维持异常。精神分裂症最像这个——内感受、身体所有权、自主感、元认知全都乱了，很难说哪一个是「因」。

**临床意义**：不同损伤模式指向不同的治疗策略。自上而下模式→认知干预（改变信念）。自下而上模式→药物/生理干预（纠正传入信号偏差）。双向循环→两个方向同时干预。

---

### 四、未来方向：八条路

1. **跨诊断进路**：不按 DSM 类别研究自我意识，而是看不同疾病共享哪些自我意识损伤维度——与 Cuthbert (2014) 的 RDoC 框架对齐。
2. **单一疾病的多过程研究**：在同一个病症里测全部六个过程，看它们之间有没有嵌套层级——哪些是最基础、受损后拖垮全体的。
3. **方法整合**：开发「自我意识测试电池」——问卷+行为实验+生理学（ERP、静息态 fMRI）+生态效度（VR）。重点是跨方法、跨层级的数据整合。
4. **神经化学基础**：多巴胺对自主感的影响已有线索（Render & Jansen, 2019）。迷幻药（赛洛西宾→自我消融）是研究自我意识神经化学的天然探针——它暂时溶解了自我/世界的边界（Bienemann et al., 2020; 2023）。
5. **跨物种比较**：自我监测几乎所有物种都有。递归元认知只有少数物种有。在演化光谱上锚定「自我意识的不同程度」，有助于理解它是否是一个必然的演化产物。
6. **机器自我意识**：探索内语言能否产生自我意识——如果给机器人装上内语言系统，它会发展出哪种自我意识？这既是哲学实验，也反过来检验人类自我意识是否真的需要叙事。
7. **意识障碍患者的自我意识测量**：对持续植物状态和昏迷患者——他们有没有残存的自我意识？新的测量工具（VR、fMRI 范式）有直接伦理意义（临终决策）。
8. **治疗转化**：更好的自我意识理论→更好的临床工具。基于过程的诊断替代基于类别的诊断。针对特定自我意识过程的干预（如内感受训练治疗焦虑，元认知训练治疗精神病）。

---

## 博导审稿

**选题想象力**：优秀。自我意识的综述太多了——但绝大多数要么只讲一个过程（如内感受），要么只讲一个疾病（如精神分裂症的自知力），要么陷入意识哲学的宏大叙事。Mograbi 的选题是独特的——以六个过程为横轴，以预测编码为竖轴，再拿临床疾病当验证网格。结果是：这张网既覆盖了体感层面的自我（内感受）又覆盖了叙事层面的自我（自传体记忆），既包容了精神分裂症的全局崩解又包容了 OCD 的单一过程异常。

**架构野心**：ISAM 是一个值得被引用的框架。不完美的框架比完美的零散观察有用得多——它给出了可证伪的预测（如果六个过程确实在预测编码框架下交互，那么自上而下的先验扭曲应在特定实验范式中产生特定的六过程响应模式）。缺点：六个过程的选择标准不够透明——为什么是这六个？为什么没有纳入自我参照加工（self-reference）、自我反思（self-reflection）、内语言（inner speech）？文章对排除了什么给出了解释（这些可能是更高阶的「结果」而非「基础」），但论证不够充分。

**临床覆盖**：非常好。表 1 是全文最有实用价值的贡献——一张六过程×十几个疾病的对照表。精神分裂症的六个过程全中不是偶然的；BPD 的情绪调节独中也不是偶然的。这张表本身就是一个有待进一步分解的研究路线图。

**方法诚意**：因为是综述而非实证论文，方法评价不合适。但文章对未来方向的建议是扎实的——跨诊断、方法整合、神经化学——每一条都对应一个具体可操作的研究策略。赛洛西宾作为自我意识探针的建议尤其及时。

**局限**：(1) 没有跨疾病的直接对比研究——这是综述的局限也是领域的局限。文章坦诚承认了。(2) PC 框架既有广度也有代价——广度在于它能套到任何事情上，代价在于它可能什么都解释得了但什么都没精确预测。(3) 神经网络细节薄弱——这篇文章没有真正讨论三网（DMN/SN/CEN）在自我意识中的角色。内感受的神经基础大量依赖岛叶和前扣带皮层（SN 的核心节点），自传体记忆和元认知大量依赖 DMN——但 SN 和 DMN 之间的动态切换以及它们如何交互产生整合的自我意识——这篇文章几乎没有涉及。

**判决：revise and resubmit（作为综述，accept with minor）。**

这是一篇好的路线图，不是一篇好的终点站。它的价值是帮研究者看清楚：自我意识不是可定位的脑区，是分布在六个过程中、由预测编码统一架构的一簇能力。它把分散的文献收拾进了一张表、一个模型、一套临床预测里。需要下一步的实证来夯实。

---

## 启发

**迁移 1：组织诊断**

自检。你的团队里有没有人是「精神分裂型结构」——六个自我意识过程全出问题型？这意味着这个人缺乏一个整合的自我叙事→在混乱中找不到稳定的自我定位→决策漂移、情绪波动、行为不一致。你看到的不是单个缺陷（只缺信心或只缺情绪调节），而是一个系统级的问题。这类人需要的不是绩效考核反馈（元认知训练）而是全面的自我叙事重建。

反之，「OCD 型结构」——只在一个过程上出问题（元认知信心低→反复检查、过度校对）——你该做的是针对性的流程干预而非全人格改组。

**迁移 2：预测编码的普适性**

PC 框架不仅讲自我意识，它回到了认知的最底层逻辑——大脑就是一个不断预测和修正的机器。你把 PC 从神经科学搬到组织管理的隐喻里也很贴切：公司也是一个持续生成「预测」（预算、战略、期望）并与「输入」（市场信号、员工反馈）比较的系统。预测误差 = 预算偏差、市场意外、员工异动 → 要么修正内部模型（调整战略）要么改变输入（改变市场条件）。这已经太远了。

**反转 1：ISAM 的层级可以倒过来吗？**

文章暗示身体自我意识是嵌套的基础——内感受是最底层的，叠上身体所有权，再叠上自主感。但反过来也成立：高阶自我意识可以自上而下地塑造身体自我意识。PTSD 患者的自传体记忆扭曲（高阶）塑造了内感受异常（低阶）——创伤记忆（高阶）让身体持续处于过度警觉（低阶内感受偏差）。如果是这样，那「嵌套层级」的网络方向就不是单向的——它是一个双向重构的环。ISAM 如果加入这个动态权重——哪些情境下自上而下权重更高、哪些情境下自下而上权重更高——会更有解释力。

**反转 2：自我意识一定要有声明的叙事吗？**

文章委婉地挑战了 Morin 的内语言理论——不排除内语言的重要性，但把它定位为结果而非基础。如果内语言不是自我意识的必要条件——那一个没有语言能力的生物（婴儿、动物、重度失语症患者）仍然可以有整合的自我意识。这个立场如果成立，会对 AI 自我意识的讨论产生直接影响：机器是否需要有「内语言」才能有自我意识？还是只要具备六个过程的信息整合架构就足够了？这是个开放问题——但文章至少给了你一个不需要先解决「机器能思考吗」就能推进的实验框架。

---

*文档生成时间：2026-06-17 | 基于 Mograbi, Hall, Arantes & Huntley (2023/2024) WIREs Cognitive Science*
*DOI: 10.1002/wcs.1670 | PMID: 38043919 | CC-BY*
"}

{"file_path": "workspace\自我意识研究\20260617T210000--paper-self-awareness-neuroscience__paper.html", "content": " 自我意识不在某个脑区里，在六个过程的交互中涌现 — Mograbi et al. 2023

自我意识不在某个脑区里，在六个过程的交互中涌现

The cognitive neuroscience of self-awareness: Current framework, clinical implications, and future research directions
作者：Daniel C. Mograbi, Simon Hall, Beatriz Arantes, Jonathan Huntley
发表：WIREs Cognitive Science, 2024 Mar-Apr; 15(2): e1670  |  DOI: 10.1002/wcs.1670  |  PMID: 38043919  |  CC-BY
自我意识预测编码内感受自主感元认知 身体所有权情绪调节自传体记忆涌现属性 跨诊断ISAM 模型

1. 问题

你拍脑门问自己：「自我意识是什么？」绝大多数人回答：「我知道我是我。」接着问：「这发生在脑的什么地方？」经典回答指向前额叶——那个最「人类」的皮层，负责元认知、自我参照、反思。但临床数据让你不安：精神分裂症的自我意识问题是全面的——身体感、自主感、元认知、自传体记忆全乱了。自闭症的内感受异常和情绪调节异常并存。抑郁症不止是自我评价降低，自传体记忆也在塌——只记得负面的、笼统的。阿尔茨海默症的自传体记忆逐片剥落。

这些病症各自的损伤模式没有显示出单一脑区的垄断。不同病变损伤的是自我意识的不同侧面，相邻而独立。

Mograbi 团队这篇综述的核心动作：不问「自我意识在哪个脑区」，问「自我意识是哪几个过程在什么框架下交互涌现的」。他们把六个过程放进预测编码的统一架构，然后拿临床神经精神疾病当验证网格。

2. 六个自我意识过程

文章梳理了六种自我意识过程，每种有不同的认知对象和神经底物。下面逐一展开。

① 内感受 Interoception

感知身体生理状态——心跳、呼吸、饱胀。自我表征最底层的材料。内隐版=稳态反射，显性版=「我心慌」。

神经基础：岛叶（特别是背侧中岛叶）→ 跨疾病内感受异常的共同汇聚点（Nord et al., 2021）

② 身体所有权 / 本体感觉

身体各部分的位置感，以及「这身体属于我」。多感官整合的产物——不是不言自明的天赋。

神经基础：顶叶（空间整合）· 前运动皮层（身体图式）· 岛叶（归属标记）· 颞顶交界（自/非自区分）

③ 自主感 Agency

体验自己是行动的发起者。传出副本（efference copy）预测行动后果→预测与反馈匹配→自主感。

神经基础：前SMA → 传出副本 · 顶叶比较器回路 · 多巴胺系统调控自主体验强度

④ 元认知 Metacognition

对自己的认知进行监测、知识和调节。具有递归性——「思考思考」。对自己能力的信心校准。

神经基础：前额叶（DLPFC、mPFC）——元认知是前额叶的专属领地

⑤ 情绪调节 Emotion Regulation

对情绪过程的思考、策略和反应。前因聚焦（认知重评）+ 反应相关（表达抑制）双轨。

神经基础：PFC（DLPFC、VMPFC）→ 杏仁核/岛叶的自上而下调控

⑥ 自传体记忆 Autobiographical Memory

回忆个人历史的记忆。事件记忆（「大二那年冬天…」）+ 个人语义（「我在X大学读书」）。

神经基础：海马（事件编码/提取）· PCC/楔前叶（自我参照）· 角回（语义整合）——重叠DMN

3. ISAM：整合自我意识模型

文章的 Figure 1 是 ISAM（Integrated Self-Awareness Model）——这是这篇文章的方法论支柱：

ISAM 三步逻辑：
① 身体自我意识（内感受 → 身体所有权 → 自主感）形成嵌套层级。内感受信号也馈入身体所有权和自主感。
② 高阶自我意识（元认知、情绪调节、自传体记忆）受内隐自下而上信号影响，但表达为显性叙事自我表征——三者之间不一定是层级而是平行交互。
③ 身体层和高阶层在两个水平上交互：具体过程层面（如内感受影响情绪调节）+ 更高整合层面 → 涌现整合的自我意识体验。

4. 临床验证：不同病症损伤不同过程

文章的核心临床贡献在 Table 1——一张六过程 × 十几个疾病的对照表。下面完整翻译，并按 PC 框架的损伤方向重新组织：

过程定义受影响的临床条件 / 症状
内感受感知全身的生理状态述情障碍 · 焦虑障碍 · ASD · 抑郁 · 功能性神经障碍 · 精神分裂症 · 进食障碍
本体感觉 / 身体所有权身体位置图和身体归属感功能性运动障碍 · 精神分裂症 · 躯体异己感 · PTSD · 人格解体障碍
自主感体验自己生成行动精神分裂症 · 异己手综合征 · 偏瘫疾病失认
元认知对认知的监测、知识和调节痴呆 · OCD · PTSD · 精神病
情绪调节对情绪的监测和调节边缘型人格障碍 · 物质滥用障碍 · 双相情感障碍 · ASD · ADHD
自传体记忆自我信息的记录（事件和个人知识）痴呆 · 解离性遗忘 · PTSD · 抑郁 · 精神分裂症
核心发现：精神分裂症是唯一的「六过程全中」疾病——内感受异常、身体所有权扭曲、自主感缺损、元认知扭曲、情绪调节受损、自传体记忆过度概括化。这不是偶然——精神分裂症的主观体验正是「我不再是我的思想与行动的主人」。PC 框架解释：这是自上而下和自下而上同时出错形成双向循环的典型案例。

5. 预测编码：统一六过程的架构

PC 不是新理论——根在 Helmholtz 的「无意识推理」，在 Friston 的自由能原理中有了数学形式化。但把 PC 系统地应用到自我意识的全部六个子过程、然后用临床数据统一验证——是这篇文章的核心架构创新。

过程自上而下的预测自下而上的感觉输入预测误差的含义
内感受对身体生理状态的信念内脏传入信号修正内感受信念或自主神经调节
身体所有权身体图式模板多感官（视觉、触觉、本体感觉）更新身体模型（如橡胶手错觉）
自主感传出副本对行动后果的预测实际感觉反馈匹配→我做的；不匹配→不是我
元认知对自己能力的信念任务表现的客观反馈更新元认知信念（信心校准）
情绪调节对情绪状态的期望和调节策略生理唤起和情感信号重新评估情境或调整调节策略
自传体记忆自我叙事框架具体回忆细节整合或过滤记忆以维持一致性

涌现：自我意识不是实体

文章反复强调一个哲学和技术上都很关键的点：自我意识是涌现属性（emergent property）。低层交互产生高层属性，这些属性不可还原为低层组分的简单相加。这意味着：

三点推论：
① 自我意识不是可定位的「东西」——它是过程，不是物件。
② 随着认知复杂度降低（往下拆），主观体验在某个还原层次消失（Dennett, 2014）。
③ 自我意识是分级现象，不是二元的——从内隐的稳态监控一直到递归的元认知，存在不同程度的自我意识。
这解释了为什么最低等的生物也能自我监控（维持内稳态），但没有高层自我意识。演化把低层的自监控机制越叠越复杂。

6. 未来方向：八条研究路线

#方向具体内容
①跨诊断进路不按 DSM 类别研究，而是按自我意识维度跨疾病分析——与 RDoC 框架对齐
②单病多过程在同一病症里测全部六个过程，看是否有嵌套层级——哪些是最基础、受损后拖垮全体的
③方法整合「自我意识测试电池」：问卷 + 行为实验 + 生理（ERP、静息态 fMRI）+ 生态效度（VR）
④神经化学多巴胺→自主感强度。赛洛西宾→自我消融——是研究自我意识神经化学的天然探针
⑤跨物种比较自我监测几乎所有物种都有。递归元认知只在少数物种。在演化光谱上锚定自我意识的「不同程度」
⑥机器自我意识内语言能否产生自我意识？机器人安装内语言系统→哪种自我意识？既是哲学实验也是神经科学检验
⑦意识障碍持续植物状态/昏迷——残存自我意识的测量（VR、fMRI），有直接伦理意义（临终决策）
⑧治疗转化基于过程的诊断替代基于类别的诊断。特定过程针对性干预：内感受训练→焦虑；元认知训练→精神病

7. 结论

这篇综述的核心移动：把「自我意识在哪个脑区」这个问题替换成「自我意识是哪几个过程在什么框架下交互涌现的」。答案是：六个过程——内感受、身体所有权、自主感、元认知、情绪调节、自传体记忆——在预测编码的统一架构下动态交互，涌现出整合自我意识。没有「自我意识脑区」——岛叶管身体感，前额叶管元认知和情绪调节，海马和 PCC 管自传体记忆——没有一个过程垄断自我意识，也没有一个脑区是总开关。

临床上：不同疾病打击不同过程，损伤方向对应 PC 框架的三种模式（自上而下、自下而上、双向循环）。精神分裂症是全局崩解的样板——六过程全有异常。抑郁症和焦虑症是自上而下主导——信念出错过滤了身体信号。OCD 可能是自下而上——边缘系统过度激活形成了扭曲的元信念。

方法上：需要跨诊断设计、单病多过程测量、问卷-行为-生理学-生态效度的四合一方法整合。赛洛西宾和跨物种比较是理解自我意识本质的两个最有力探针。

哲学上：自我意识是涌现属性，不是实体。它是过程，不是物件。它是分级的，不是二元的。它是动态的，不是固化的。

自我意识不在某个脑区里，在六个过程的交互中涌现

The cognitive neuroscience of self-awareness — Mograbi, Hall, Arantes & Huntley (2023/2024)
作者：Daniel C. Mograbi (PUC-Rio / KCL), Simon Hall (Camden & Islington NHS), Beatriz Arantes (PUC-Rio), Jonathan Huntley (UCL / Exeter)
期刊：WIREs Cognitive Science, 2024, 15(2): e1670  |  性质：综述 (Advanced Review)  |  DOI: 10.1002/wcs.1670

1. 问题

你拍脑门问自己：「自我意识是什么？」绝大多数人会回答：「我知道我是我。」你追问：「这发生在脑的什么地方？」经典回答指向前额叶——那个最「人类」的皮层。但看了一圈临床数据你会感到不安：精神分裂症的自我意识问题是全面的——身体感、自主感、元认知、自传体记忆全乱了。自闭症的内感受异常和情绪调节异常并存。抑郁症的不止是自我评价降低，自传体记忆也在塌。边缘型人格障碍的情绪调节彻底崩。阿尔茨海默症的自传体记忆逐片剥落。

所以我们可能问错了问题。不问「自我意识在哪个脑区」，问「自我意识是哪几个过程在什么框架下交互涌现的」。Mograbi 团队这篇文章，就是来回答这个问题的。

2. 六大自我意识过程

文章梳理了六种自我意识过程，每种有不同的认知对象和神经底物。关键区分：身体自我意识（内感受→身体所有权→自主感，形成嵌套层级——内感受信号也馈入身体所有权和自主感）和高阶自我意识（元认知、情绪调节、自传体记忆，平行交互但受内隐自下而上信号影响）。

六个过程的选择标准：有明确的神经生物学基础 + 演化视角（在其它物种中存在较不复杂的版本）。文章明确排除了自我参照、自我反思、内语言——它们可能是更高阶的「结果」而非「基础过程」。内语言可能在高阶自我意识中重要，但不是必要条件——没有语言的生物（婴儿、动物、重度失语症患者）仍可有整合的自我意识。

内感受三个维度

维度定义测量方法
内感受精度 (Accuracy)客观上检测到内脏信号的准确性心跳检测任务、胃胀感判断
内感受敏感度 (Sensibility)自己主观评估自己多擅长感知身体问卷（MAIA）、自我报告
内感受觉察 (Awareness)精度与敏感度的匹配度——元认知性质的指标自信度-准确度对应（Garfinkel et al., 2015）

自主感的双通路

Renes et al. (2013) 的经典实验证明：精神分裂症患者在显性（有意识控制方块位置）条件下自主感正常，但在 17ms 阈下启动的隐性通路中显著缺失。这说明自主感有两条路——隐性的传出副本比较回路和显性的有意识推断回路——精神分裂症先断裂的是隐性通路，这比显性通路更基础。

元认知的三域

MAS 域定义精神分裂症损伤
理解自己的心智自我归因、信心校准、自知力信念不恰当确信→妄想；缺乏自知力
理解他人的心智推断他人情绪和意图情绪推断准确性下降→社交功能差
掌控 (Mastery)调节心理状态的策略策略贫乏→社交图式贫乏→恶性循环

情绪调节的两条路

策略类型包含什么各疾病偏好
前因聚焦 (Antecedent-focused)选择情境、修正情境、注意分配、认知重评抑郁：认知重评利用不足
反应相关 (Response-related)反应抑制、攻击、物质滥用社交焦虑：过度依赖表达抑制

自传体记忆的连续谱

事件记忆（「大二那年冬天我跟朋友……」）和个人语义（「我在 X 大学读书」）不是二元对立——大多数记忆落在两者之间。但不同检索方式对应的神经底物不同（Burianova & Grady, 2007），这在临床上有意义：复发性抑郁的自传体记忆是笼统的、过度概括化的——对正面和中性刺激难以提取具体回忆，对负面记忆则过度聚焦。这与负面自我信念相互强化——自传体记忆既是抑郁的因（笼统的记忆无法提供正面自我参照的证据）也是果（负面自我概念过滤记忆提取）。

3. 预测编码：统一的认知架构

文章的核心架构选择是把所有六个过程放进预测编码。这个框架的根在 Helmholtz 的「无意识推理」概念，在 Friston 的自由能原理那里得到了数学形式化。但把 PC 系统地应用到自我意识的所有六个子过程——这是新的。

PC 讲自我意识的核心逻辑：大脑持续生成自上而下的预测 (priors)，与自下而上的感觉信息比较。不匹配 = 预测误差。最小化预测误差有两个方向：修正信念（更新内部自我模型）或改变输入（通过行动改变世界/身体状态）。六个过程都在做同一件事——只是输入来源不同。

ISAM：整合自我意识模型

文章 Figure 1 的 ISAM（Integrated Self-Awareness Model）有三个核心主张：

  1. 身体自我意识的嵌套层级——内感受信号馈入身体所有权，身体所有权馈入自主感判断。形成嵌套但不完全层级。
  2. 高阶自我意识受内隐信号影响但表达为显性叙事自我表征——元认知/情绪调节/自传体记忆之间不一定是层级关系，而是平行交互。
  3. 身体自我和高阶自我在过程层和整合层两个层次交互——最终涌现出整合的自我意识体验。这不是任何一个过程的产物，也不是任何一个脑区的活动。

涌现而非实体

关键观点：自我意识是涌现属性——低层交互产生高层属性，这些属性不可还原为低层组分的简单相加。这意味着：(1) 自我意识不是可定位的「东西」——它是过程，不是物件；(2) 随着认知复杂度降低（往下拆），主观体验在某个还原层次消失在计算中（Dennett, 2014）；(3) 自我意识是分级现象，不是二元的——从内隐的稳态监控一直到递归的元认知。最低等生物也能自我监控但没有高层自我意识；演化把低层自监控机制越叠越复杂，最终涌现出人类级别的反身思维。

4. 临床验证：不同疾病打击不同过程

精神分裂症是最全面的打击——六个过程几乎全有异常。这解释了这个病症特殊的主观体验：「我不再是我的思想和行动的主人」。岛叶背侧中部是一个跨诊断会聚点——多个精神疾病的内感受异常都在这里找到共同神经信号（Nord et al., 2021）。

临床意义：不同损伤模式指向不同治疗策略。自上而下模式→认知干预；自下而上模式→药物/生理干预；双向循环→两个方向同时干预。抑郁症用 CBT（改变负面信念→修正内感受解释），AD 目前只能延缓自下而上的记忆系统衰退，精神分裂症需要综合方案。

5. 未来方向：八条路

文章给了八条路，我挑最值得注意的三条说：

④ 赛洛西宾作为自我意识探针：赛洛西宾导致「自我消融」(ego-dissolution)——自我参照降低、自我与世界边界瓦解。这是一种可逆的、剂量依赖的自我意识暂时改变。它提供了一个天然探针：如果你暂时干扰了预测编码中的自上而下先验（自我模型），六个过程如何分别受到影响？这个实验伦理上可以做，理论上直接检验 ISAM 的框架预测。
⑥ 机器自我意识：文章提出——如果给机器人装上内语言系统，它会发展出哪种自我意识？这既是哲学实验（检验内语言是否是自我意识的充分条件），也反过来检验人类自我意识是否真的需要叙事。如果机器人有内语言但没有自我意识，那 Morin (2018) 的内语言理论就裂了。如果机器人有内语言并且真的涌现了某种自我意识——那我们对「谁是自我意识拥有者」的定义就得彻底改变。

6. 结论

自我意识不是前额叶的广播台（这个你已经从 Deco 2021 知道了），也不是任何一个脑区的专属活动。Mograbi 2023/2024 补上了另一半：自我意识是六个过程——内感受、身体所有权、自主感、元认知、情绪调节、自传体记忆——在预测编码框架下动态交互中涌现的分级现象。它不是可定位的「东西」，不是单一脑区的活动，不是二元的开关。最低等生物有内隐自监控但没有高层自我意识——演化把低层自监控机制越叠越复杂，最终在人类这里涌现出了递归的反身思维。

这篇文章在你目录里的位置：
Bressler & Menon (2010) 给了你三网动力学——DMN 自我系统、SN 调度系统、CEN 任务引擎。
Deco et al. (2021) 用信息流告诉你：全局工作空间不在前额叶，在 DMN 的中线皮层——楔前叶/PCC/扣带回峡是跨任务不变的「收件箱」。
Mograbi et al. (2023/2024) 告诉你：这个「收件箱」接收的是六个不同来源的信件——身体、动作、认知、情感、记忆——而且每个来源的信件格式不同，但都经过同一套预测编码的「核对」。自我意识不来自收件箱的位置，来自核对的过程。

博导审稿

选题想象力：优秀。自我意识的综述太多了——但绝大多数要么只讲一个过程（如内感受），要么只讲一个疾病，要么陷入意识哲学的宏大叙事。Mograbi 的选题是独特的——以六个过程为横轴，以预测编码为竖轴，再拿临床疾病当验证网格。结果是：这张网覆盖了从体感层面的自我（内感受）到叙事层面的自我（自传体记忆），既能解释精神分裂症的全局崩解，又能解释 OCD 的单一过程异常。

架构野心：ISAM 是一个值得被引用的框架。不完美的框架比完美的零散观察有用得多——它给出了可证伪的预测。缺点：六个过程的选择标准不够透明——为什么是这六个？为什么没有纳入自我参照加工和内语言？文章对排除了什么给出了解释（这些可能是更高阶的「结果」而非「基础」），但论证稍显不足。神经网络细节薄弱——这篇文章几乎没有深入讨论 DMN/SN/CEN 在六个过程中的角色。

临床覆盖：表 1 是全文最有实用价值的贡献——一张六过程×十几个疾病的对照表。精神分裂症的六个过程全中不是偶然的，BPD 的情绪调节独中也不是偶然的。

局限：(1) 没有跨疾病的直接对比研究——综述的局限也是领域的局限。(2) PC 框架既有广度也有代价——广度在于它能套到任何事情上，代价在于它可能什么都解释得了但什么都没精确预测。(3) 缺乏神经网络的深层讨论。

判决：这是一篇好的路线图，不是好的终点站。它的价值是帮研究者看清楚：自我意识不是可定位的脑区，是分布在六个过程中、由预测编码统一架构的一簇能力。需要下一步的实证来夯实。

启发

迁移：你公司里有没有「精神分裂型结构」——六个自我意识过程全出问题型？这意味着这个人缺乏一个整合的自我叙事→在混乱中找不到稳定的自我定位→决策漂移、情绪波动、行为不一致。这类人需要的不是绩效考核反馈（元认知训练）而是全面的自我叙事重建。反之，「OCD 型结构」——只在一个过程上出问题（信心低→反复检查）——你该做的是针对性的流程干预而非全人格改组。

混搭：PC 框架不仅讲自我意识。公司也是一个持续生成「预测」（预算、战略、期望）并与「输入」（市场信号、员工反馈）比较的系统。预测误差 = 预算偏差、市场意外 → 要么修正内部模型（调整战略）要么改变输入（改变市场条件）。但「元认知」的对等物——一个理解自己预测校准程度的系统——在大多数组织里完全缺失。

反转：文章暗示身体自我意识是嵌套的基础——内感受是最底层的。但反过来也成立：高阶自我意识可以自上而下塑造身体自我意识。PTSD 患者——自传体记忆扭曲（高阶）→ 身体持续过度警觉（低阶内感受偏差）。ISAM 如果加入动态权重——哪些情境下自上而下权重更高、哪些情境下自下而上更高——解释力会更强。另外：如果内语言不是自我意识的必要条件，那机器是否需要有「内语言」才能有自我意识？还是只要具备六个过程的信息整合架构就足够了？这是个开放问题——但文章的框架至少给了你一个不需要先解决「机器能思考吗」就能推进的实验方法。

文档生成时间：2026-06-17  |  基于 Mograbi, Hall, Arantes & Huntley (2023/2024) WIREs Cognitive Science
DOI: 10.1002/wcs.1670  |  PMID: 38043919  |  CC-BY  |  全文 15 页
"}

---

### 这篇文章到底在说什么

一句话：**自我意识不是任何一个脑区的东西，是六个过程在预测编码框架下交互涌现的分级现象。**

六个过程：
- **身体自我**（嵌套层级）：内感受 → 身体所有权 → 自主感
- **高阶自我**（平行交互）：元认知 · 情绪调节 · 自传体记忆

统一架构：预测编码。自上而下先验 ⇄ 自下而上感觉输入 → 比较 → 误差最小化。六个过程全用这套逻辑，但输入来源不同（内脏/多感官/运动反馈/任务反馈/生理唤起/记忆痕迹）。

临床验证：不同疾病打击不同过程组合——精神分裂症六个全中，BPD 情绪调节独损，AD 自传体记忆+元认知，OCD 元认知单点异常。没有一个脑区是总开关。

### HTML 里 6 张图

| 图 | 内容 |
|----|------|
| 图 0 | 总览：ISAM 核心框架 |
| 图 1 | 六大过程完整对比（定义·神经基础·临床疾病） |
| 图 2 | 预测编码如何统一六个过程——六格的实例化矩阵 |
| 图 3 | 临床损伤矩阵——六过程×七疾病的红点热图 + 三种 PC 损伤模式 |
| 图 4 | 未来研究八大方向 |
| 终图 | 三篇论文的关系：Bressler 2010（三网架构）→ Deco 2021（信息归集点）→ Mograbi 2024（多过程涌现机制） |

### 与你目录中已有材料的关系

你目录现在三篇源论文齐了，形成了一条递进线索：

> **Bressler & Menon 2010**：自我意识在哪几张网上（DMN/SN/CEN）→ **Deco 2021**：全局工作空间具体在哪（中线皮层，不是前额叶）→ **Mograbi 2024**：这些位置上的自我意识是怎么从多个过程中涌现出来的（预测编码统一六个过程）
